title: Compliance isn’t a checkbox. It’s the architecture.
intro: Protecting patient information isn’t something we bolt on at the end. It’s designed into every workflow, every device, and every person from day one. Here’s exactly how we safeguard PHI — and why practices trust us with it.

heroChecks: Encryption in transit and at rest
Human review on AI-assisted clinical & billing outputs

trustStats.label: Named training modules — 80% minimum to pass
Company-managed devices, MFA + VPN by default
Supervisor ratio target with daily check-ins
Signed before PHI access — directly accountable
Internal root-cause review target on Level 1 events

safeguards.title: Administrative
safeguards.desc: The policies and people that govern how PHI is handled — and who is accountable.

safeguards.title: Technical
safeguards.desc: The systems that control access to PHI and protect it from unauthorized eyes.
items: Encryption in transit and at rest
MFA and mandatory VPN on every login

safeguards.title: Physical
safeguards.desc: Control over the devices and environments where work actually happens.
items: Secure disposal of records and devices

controls.title: Workforce training
controls.body: Our standard process has each team member complete an eight-module HIPAA and compliance program with an 80% minimum to pass — refreshed annually and on regulatory change.

controls.title: Access controls
controls.body: Access to PHI is role-based and least-privilege. Each person has a unique ID; permissions are reviewed and revoked promptly once they’re no longer needed.

controls.title: Encryption
controls.body: PHI is encrypted in transit and at rest using industry-standard protocols, so data is protected both as it moves and where it lives.

controls.title: Audit logs
controls.body: PHI access is logged. Records are retained for six years and reviewed for anomalies — accountability you can audit.

controls.title: Secure communication
controls.body: PHI travels through approved, secured channels. Our standard process does not route patient information through unsecured email or consumer messaging tools.

controls.title: Minimum Necessary Rule
controls.body: We use and disclose the minimum PHI reasonably required for the task at hand — including in AI prompts, which are reviewed for PHI minimization before use.

baResp.title: Signed BAA first
baResp.body: Our standard onboarding process executes a Business Associate Agreement before PHI access.

baResp.title: Direct HIPAA liability
baResp.body: As a Business Associate we’re directly liable under the law, not just to you by contract.

baResp.title: Human accountability for AI
baResp.body: When AI assists with clinical or billing work, a human reviews the output before finalization and the accountability is ours — “the AI did it” is not an answer.

baResp.title: Vendor BAAs on file
baResp.body: Any subcontractor or tool that could touch PHI signs its own BAA before we use it.

incident.title: Detect
incident.body: Monitoring and audit logs surface anomalies fast — often before they become incidents.

incident.title: Contain
incident.body: Access is locked down immediately to stop any potential exposure from spreading.

incident.title: Assess
incident.body: An internal root-cause review, targeted within 24 hours of a Level 1 event: what did the system allow?

incident.title: Notify
incident.body: We notify you promptly, with the facts and the plan. Contractual breach-notification timelines are set in your BAA.

incident.title: Remediate & review
incident.body: We fix the root cause, update the SOP, and feed the lesson back into training.

monitoring.title: Quarterly AI review
monitoring.body: Every AI tool’s security posture and BAA currency is reviewed at least quarterly by our CTO.

monitoring.title: Annual recertification
monitoring.body: Workforce compliance training is refreshed annually and on regulatory change.

monitoring.title: Designed-in checkpoints
monitoring.body: New workflows are designed to include compliance validation — not audited on afterward.

monitoring.title: Measured like any KPI
monitoring.body: PHI exposure is treated as a top-priority risk, tracked on dashboards with named owners.
